name: Turkey (the bird)
description: Wild and domesticated turkeys, gobbles, and Thanksgiving lore.

questions.q: Which scientific name designates the wild turkey?

source.notes: Meleagris ocellata is the ocellated turkey of the Yucatán.

questions.q: How many living species of turkey are there?

source.notes: Wild turkey (M. gallopavo) and ocellated turkey (M. ocellata).

questions.q: What is a baby turkey called?
What is a male turkey called?
What is a female turkey called?
What is the dangling, fleshy red flap of skin under a turkey's chin called?

source.notes: The 'snood' is the fleshy bit that hangs over the beak; the 'caruncle' is the bumpy red skin on neck.

questions.q: Which Founding Father reportedly preferred the turkey to the bald eagle as the U.S. national bird?

source.notes: Mentioned in his 1784 letter to his daughter — but he never formally proposed it for the seal.

questions.q: Which U.S. president began the modern tradition of pardoning a Thanksgiving turkey?

source.notes: The first official pardon was in 1989. Lincoln spared a turkey for his son Tad in 1863, but it wasn't an annual ceremony.

questions.q: Where were turkeys first domesticated, around 2,000 years ago?

source.notes: Indigenous Mesoamericans domesticated wild turkeys ~800 BCE - 100 BCE.

questions.q: Why is the bird (Meleagris gallopavo) called 'turkey' in English?
choices: Mistaken trade route confusion with Turkish guinea fowl
It was named after Sultan Suleyman
It crosses the Bosporus during migration

source.notes: Europeans confused American turkeys with similar-looking guinea fowl imported via Ottoman 'Turkey merchants.'

questions.q: On which Thursday is U.S. Thanksgiving — when most turkeys are eaten — celebrated?
Approximately how many turkeys are eaten in the U.S. on Thanksgiving Day?
Which amino acid in turkey is famously (incorrectly) blamed for post-Thanksgiving sleepiness?

source.notes: Turkey doesn't contain more tryptophan than other meats — the post-meal sleepiness is more about overeating carbs.

questions.q: Roughly how fast can a wild turkey run?

source.notes: Wild turkeys can run ~25 mph and fly short distances at ~55 mph.

questions.q: Can wild turkeys fly?
choices: Yes, for hundreds of miles in migration

source.notes: They roost in trees at night. Domestic turkeys, bred to be heavy, can't fly.

questions.q: Which Looney Tunes road runner-style cartoon turkey is voiced as a Texas-style 'big mouth'?

source.notes: Foghorn is technically a rooster/leghorn, not a turkey, but his style draws on Southern bird humor common to barnyards.

questions.q: Which 1972 Bowery Hill song-style holiday classic mentions 'the turkey is no longer cold'?

source.notes: Arlo Guthrie's 'Alice's Restaurant Massacree' is a Thanksgiving radio tradition since 1965.

questions.q: What do you call a group of turkeys?

source.notes: A 'rafter' or sometimes a 'gang' of turkeys.

questions.q: Which U.S. state is the largest turkey producer?

source.notes: Minnesota produces ~40M+ turkeys/year — the most of any state.

questions.q: What sound does a male turkey famously make to attract mates?

source.notes: Only males gobble; females make clucks and yelps.
